news.title: Average US long-term mortgage rate leaps to 6.38%, the highest level in more than six months
news.para: American home loan interest rates have surged to their highest point in over six months. This rise is making it more expensive for people to buy homes. The 30-year fixed mortgage rate is now 6.38%. This is happening during the peak home buying season. Higher oil prices and inflation worries are contributing factors. Borrowing costs for refinancing also increased.

news.title: Noida airport to spur real estate growth across NCR, boost demand along Yamuna Expressway
news.para: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated phase-I of the Noida International Airport at Jewar, a ₹11,200 crore project poised to reshape NCR's real estate. This development is expected to significantly boost connectivity across western Uttar Pradesh and NCR, driving demand across residential, commercial, and logistics segments, and attracting institutional investment.

news.title: Amravati makes cool roofs mandatory to beat extreme heat
news.para: Amravati Municipal Corporation mandates cool roofs for new buildings and offers tax incentives for existing ones to combat extreme heat and reduce energy demand. This initiative, integrating heat mitigation into urban planning, aims to lower indoor temperatures and enhance climate resilience. The policy follows a study revealing high surface temperatures in urban areas.

news.title: Prime minister inaugurates phase-I of Noida International Airport
news.para: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the first phase of the Noida International Airport in Jewar on Saturday. Developed at an estimated cost of Rs 11,200 crore, the airport is designed as a multi-modal transport hub with an initial passenger handling capacity of 12 million per annum, expandable to 70 million.

news.title: Mohali development body launches 50th affordable housing project in sector 114
news.para: GMADA has launched its 50th affordable housing project, a 25-acre plotted colony named "White City" in Sector 114, Mohali. This initiative aims to provide home ownership opportunities for lower and middle-income groups. Mohali currently boasts over 50 approved affordable residential colonies, with more in development.

news.title: Noida airport phase-I cost ₹11,282 crore, total project pegged at ₹29,560 crore
news.para: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the first phase of Noida International Airport at Jewar on Saturday. This Rs 11,282 crore project aims to make Jewar a key aviation hub for North India. The airport will feature a cargo terminal and a new MRO facility. Uttar Pradesh will become the first state with five international airports.

news.title: Five years late, Haryana RERA asks Sunrays Heights to hand over home with 11% interest
news.para: Haryana Rera has ordered Sunrays Heights Private Limited to give possession of flats to buyers. This comes after a five-year delay in the affordable housing project 63 Golf Drive. Allottees will receive applicable interest for the delay. The authority rejected the developer's reasons for the delay. This ruling protects homebuyers' rights for timely project delivery.

news.title: Jan Vishwas Bill: New Property tax framework proposed for NDMC areas
news.para: New property tax rules are coming for New Delhi Municipal Council areas. The government plans a complete overhaul of the tax structure. A Municipal Valuation Committee will classify properties and fix base values. Property Identification Codes will be mandatory for tax payments and civic services. Penalties for tax evasion will be linked to the amount evaded.

news.title: Spain's proposed 100% non-EU property tax stalls in congress
news.para: Spain's plan to tax non-EU property buyers up to 100 percent faces hurdles. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's minority government struggles to secure votes. Allies are divided, and elections loom in 2027. Foreign buyers represented 20 percent of purchases last year. The proposed tax aimed to ease competition for local buyers amid a housing shortage.

news.title: Home registrations in Kolkata dip 1% year-on-year to 4,974  in February 2026
news.para: In February, the Kolkata Metropolitan Area saw 4,974 home sales, a slight dip of 1% from last year's 5,016.  The sweet spot for buyers was apartments between 501 and 1,000 sq ft, which made up a much larger 58% of sales, a significant jump from 44% in the previous year.

news.title: Delhi: Over 700 DDA flats booked within two days of launch
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority's housing projects in Narela have seen over 700 flats booked within a mere two days of their release. Both the 'DDA Karmayogi Awaas Yojana' and the 'DDA Nagrik Awaas Yojana' are drawing considerable attention, signaling Narela's emerging reputation as an attractive neighborhood for potential residents.

news.title: Residential encroachments of upto 500 sq ft to be regularised for free in Maharashtra except Mumbai
news.para: The Maharashtra government is taking a step towards stability by allowing the regularisation of residential encroachments up to 500 square feet, provided they existed before January 1, 2011. It's essential to note that Mumbai city, along with its suburbs, is excluded from this policy.

news.title: Over 31,000 houses completed in J&K under PMAY-U, delays linked beneficiary constraints: Government
news.para: Jammu and Kashmir has completed over 31,000 homes under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana. Many more are under construction, with the extended deadline set for September 2026. Delays are mainly due to beneficiaries' financial constraints and rising material costs. The government is now offering interest-free loans to help complete these houses.

news.title: Centre to remove one-year imprisonment provision for allottees for non-compliance with realty norms
news.para: Homebuyers receive good news as the Centre plans to remove jail time for non-compliance with Real Estate Appellate Tribunal orders. A new proposal suggests replacing imprisonment with a penalty. This penalty will be a maximum of ten percent of the property's cost. The Jan Vishwas Amendment Provisions Bill 2026 introduced this change.

news.title: Ludhiana civic body yet to recover dues from one lakh property taxpayers
news.para: Ludhiana Municipal Corporation faces a shortfall in property tax collection, needing to recover dues from 1 lakh taxpayers to meet its Rs 160 crore target. Officials are working weekends and holidays to boost recovery, while also anticipating challenges like election duties and census activities impacting future collections.

news.title: Redeveloped flat can't be taxable as 'other income': ITAT
news.para: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal in Mumbai has ruled in favour of a taxpayer. The taxpayer received a flat worth Rs 11.7 crore for surrendering tenancy rights. The tribunal held this transaction as a transfer of a capital asset. The taxpayer is eligible for exemption under Section 54F of the Income Tax Act.

news.title: Pune civic body links building permission to land acquisition for road widening
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation (PMC) has mandated developers to submit land proposals for road widening within 15 days of seeking building permission. Land must be physically handed over before a new building reaches the plinth level to address the city's road infrastructure deficit and ease traffic congestion on arterial roads.

news.title: Notandas Realty strengthens its legacy of craftsmanship with ultra-modern luxury innovation
news.para: Notandas Realty has carved a niche as a top-tier luxury developer within Mumbai’s fast-paced real estate landscape. Boasting close to two decades of industry insight, the company specializes in stunning homes and high-end commercial areas. Guided by the visionary Mr. Harsh Jagwani, Notandas Realty leverages modern technology to ensure superior service and operational excellence.

news.title: Birla Estates, Arvind SmartSpaces enter Mumbai’s redevelopment segment with new housing projects
news.para: Birla Estates and Arvind SmartSpaces are expanding into Mumbai's property market. They have launched new redevelopment projects in Khar West and Santacruz. These ventures tap into the city's demand for housing. These projects represent significant revenue potential for both companies.

news.title: Madhuri Dixit leases commercial property in Mumbai for ₹2.81 crore
news.para: Bollywood star Madhuri Dixit has leased a commercial space in Mumbai's One Lodha Place for a substantial monthly rent starting at ₹4.25 lakh. The five-year agreement, registered in March 2026, includes a 67.91 sq meter unit and parking.  Rent escalates annually, culminating in a total lease value of ₹2.81 crore over the term.
